Motor Vehicle Accident SOP

Standard Operating Procedure — ClaimOS Reference

Purpose

This SOP provides a clear, standardized workflow for reviewing MVA claims — from initial patient pursuit through final payment resolution.

1Validate payer information — confirm insurance, subscriber ID, claim number, and coverage details.
2Submit medical bills — via e-submit, fax, or mail depending on payer setup.
3Follow up with payers — call or email for claim status at defined intervals.
4Manage claim status — update Case Page with correct status and next review date.
5Resolve claims — handle payments, denials, appeals, or returns as appropriate.

Scope

Applies to all MVA employees responsible for reviewing medical billing accounts, contacting payers, documenting account activity, and resolving claim issues.

Key Systems

Tool / SystemPurpose
Enforcer 360Review patient accounts, claim status, and billing records
UB-04 / CMS 1500Facility or hospital billing forms
Nitro PDFEditing and reviewing billing documents
Payer Portals / ZoomOutbound call and portal access

Claim Status Reference

StatusRequired Action
Claim ReceivedConfirm receipt with payer and verify processing timeline
Pending PaymentMonitor progress, follow up if timeline exceeded
PaidConfirm amount, close or reconcile account
DeniedIdentify denial reason, determine resubmission or appeal
Claim Not ReceivedResubmit claim, confirm transmission with payer

Patient Pursuit

Contacting patients to gather accident and insurance details

When leaving a voicemail, do not leave any sensitive information. Only provide: your name, facility name, callback number, and Claim ID as reference.

Voicemail Script

"Good Morning/Afternoon. My name is _____ and I am calling from [Facility]. If you could please return my call at (615) 694-8131 ext 992566 and please reference our Claim ID _________. Thank you and have a wonderful day."

Scenario 1 — Patient Available, Details Provided

"Hello. My name is _____ and I am calling on behalf of [Facility]. This call is being recorded for quality assurance. May I please speak with [Patient Name]?"

After ID: "For security purposes, can you please confirm your Date of Birth?"

If DOB not confirmed: Advise a letter will be sent on file. If DOB wrong: End call, advise they can expect mail.

Questions to Ask

  • How many vehicles involved? (SMVA / MMVA / PMVA)
  • Date of the accident?
  • Were you the driver, passenger, or pedestrian?
  • Were you at fault?
  • If NOT at fault → T/P insurance company, policyholder name, claim filed?
  • If AT fault → your auto insurance name and claim number
  • Health insurance provider, Policy #, Group #, Member ID
  • If MMVA → attorney represented? (Law firm, attorney name, email, phone, fax, address)

Sample Notation

"Outbound call placed to [Patient] at [Phone]. Spoke with patient confirming 2 vehicles involved — MMVA, DOA: 03/30/26. Patient was driver, not at fault. T/P: [Name], Insurance: USAA, Claim #: 015651784808. HI: Cigna, Member ID: U2555070801. Attorney represented: Steven Nunez 210-269-0599. Moving to Validation Pending — Level 1. NRD — 1 Day."

Case Page After Scenario 1

  • Account Status: Validation Pending — Level 1
  • Review Date: 1 Day

Scenario 2 — Due Diligence (Health Insurance Available)

If 2 questionnaires have been sent and patient is unresponsive, activate HI. Move to CBSP. NRD: 1 Day.

Scenario 3 — Due Diligence (Bill Filed / Bill POR)

If HI unavailable but FP and TP available — activate TP. Move to BSP. NRD: 1 Day.

Scenario 3 — Due Diligence (No Payer)

Go to Options → Return Claim. Reason: "No Form of Ins. Avail. — Patient Responsibility."

Payer Validation

Validating First Party, Third Party, and Attorney contacts

Reviewing the Account

On the MVA Tab, review reimbursement paths: Primary = First Party, Secondary = Patient's Health (do not call), Tertiary = Third Party, Attorney Info if applicable.

First Party Auto Payer Contact

"Hello, this is [Your Name] calling from [Facility]. This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes. I'm calling to see if a patient has filed a claim."

Confirm the Following

  • Date of Injury • Claim Number • Policy Number
  • Payer Location (billing address) • Adjuster info (name, address, phone, fax, email)
  • Claim type: MedPay / PIP / UM/UIM
  • Patient role: Driver / Passenger / Pedestrian | At Fault / Not At Fault
  • Claim status: Open or Closed (reason if closed)
  • SMVA / MMVA / PMVA
"Called First Party Liberty Mutual at 800-500-7044 and spoke with Tim. Confirmed DOI and claim details. Med Pay claim is open. Patient was not at fault driver in MMVA. TPL is listed as GEICO but no claim info given. Patient has not retained an attorney."

Third Party Auto Payer Contact

"Hello, this is [Your Name] calling from [Facility]. This call may be recorded. I'm calling to see if a patient has filed a claim."

Same confirmation fields as FP, but claim type = Bodily Injury (BI).

"Called TPL USAA at 800-123-4567 and spoke with Heather. Confirmed DOI and claim details. BI claim is open. Patient was not at fault in MMVA. FP is Liberty Mutual but no claim info given. Patient has retained an attorney: Lawman Campbell at 888-111-1234."

Attorney Contact

"Hello, this is [Your Name] from [Facility]. Our records indicate your firm represents [Patient Name] for a Motor Vehicle accident. Could you please confirm?"

Request: Letter of Representation and Signed Medical Authorization — fax to 615-486-6518 or email MVA@enablecomp.com.

Account Status After Validation

ValidatedStatusReview
FP validatedValidation Pending — Level 21 Day
TP validatedValidation Pending — Level 21 Day
Attorney confirmedValidation Pending — Level 21 Day
Voicemail leftValidation Pending — Level 12 Days

Bill Submission Pending

Submitting bills to payers after validation

Check the Timely Filing Date on the MVA tab. If deadline is within 10 days (or shown in red/passed), add to escalation tracker for "missed deadline" and continue billing auto insurance.

MVA Tab Checklist Before Submitting

  • ✅ Payer Selection & Payer Location
  • ✅ Reimbursement Path
  • ✅ Subscriber Name (first and last)
  • ✅ Subscriber ID
  • ✅ Relationship
  • ✅ Follow-up Date & Status

Documents to Upload

Go to Options → Attach UB/1500 to auto-generate and upload. Verify AOB is present (check Reports → MVA → Accident Link Analysis if missing).

  • PIP states: Medical records required (auto-uploaded on claim import). Only required for PIP claims.
  • MedPay: No medical records required — only AOB and UB/1500.

First Party Submission — E-Submit

If E-Submit button is present → click it → select all applicable documents → attach.

First Party Submission — By Fax

1Open Submit tab in Enforcer. Enter payer fax number in Ready for Faxing field.
2Put payer's claim number in subject line (use Subscriber ID if claim # not in header).
3Use appropriate template. Include patient name in Fax Body field.
4Select your team in Team Mailbox. Select "Claim" in Fax Type dropdown.
5Click Fax → select all applicable documents → attach.

After Submission — Case Page Update

  • Review Date: 30 days from submission
  • Status FP: First Party Claim — Bill Filed
  • Status TPL: Third Party Claim — Bill POR
  • Complete Initial Bill Date / Bill POR Date field

Notation (Note Type: Enter Claim Data into Enforcer — 18)

Include: payer billed, submission type (fax/mail/e-submit), documents submitted.

1st Party / Bill Filed

Follow-up on First Party auto insurance claims

Do NOT work accounts that have Attorney representation on file.

Scenario 1 — Bill Received / Processing

"Hello, this is [Name] calling from [Facility]. This call may be recorded. I'm calling to see if there is a claim filed for a mutual patient."

Confirm: When received? Status? Documents needed? Adjuster info? Attorney on file? Expected timeline?

"Called Geico at 800-413-4153. Spoke with Rick. Claim #451235718 received 11/25/2024, currently processing."
  • Status: Bill Filed | Review: 30 Days

Scenario 2 — Pending Payment

Ask: Expected payment amount? Expected payment date? Adjuster info?

"Called State Farm at 713-456-1145. Spoke with Amy. Claim #59178168 received 10/24/2024. Set to pay $447.30 on 11/04/2024."
  • MVA Status: FP Potential Payer | Status: Bill Filed | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 3 — Paid

Obtain: Check Number, Check Amount, Check Date, Facility paid to.

"Called Allstate at 800-414-4616. Spoke with Heather. Claim #5612689151 received 11/01/2024. Paid $506.16 on 11/10/2024 with check #5697134 to [Facility]."
  • MVA Status: FP Paid | Status: Payment Mailed | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 4 — Denied

Get: Denial reason, request EOB/Denial Letter to MVA@enablecomp.com or fax 615-873-0959, appeal deadline, appeal address/fax.

"Called The General at 879-146-5519. Spoke with John. Claim #78185058 denied 10/02/2024 for exhausted medical benefits. Requested exhaust letter to fax 615-486-6518."
  • Status: EOB Requested | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 5 — Bill Not Received

Ask: Timely filing date? Billing mailing address and fax number? Confirm correct address on file.

"Called Mercury at 777-888-1111. Spoke with Junita. No claim on file for DOS. Confirmed address correct. Fax corrected from 815-444-5164 to 514-444-6131. Sending back to onshore to rebill."
  • Status: Request ReBill — Initial | Review: 1 Day

Commercial Bill Filed

Follow-up on health/commercial insurance claims

Review the MVA Tab for Health Insurance reimbursement paths: Commercial or Government. Check Notes Tab to verify payer before calling.

Scenario 1 — Processing

Confirm: Date received? Claim number? Status? Documents needed? Expected timeline?

"Called BCBS at 800-800-800. Spoke with Leslie. Bill received 1/3/25. Claim #BC01-5689. Currently processing — allow 30 days."
  • MVA Status: COM Check Claim Status | Status: Commercial Bill Filed | Review: 30 Days

Scenario 2 — Pending Payment

Ask: Recommended allowance amount? Expected payment date?

  • Status: Commercial Bill Filed | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 3 — Paid

Obtain: Check Number, Check Amount, Check Date, Facility paid to.

"Called HI Aetna. Spoke with Ray. Claim KJ2RD71 received 01/14/26, processed 02/07/26. Paid $8,613.74. Draft #1234208238 via EFT to account ending 5979. Ref: RAYM03132026."
  • MVA Status: COM Paid | Status: Payment Mailed | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 4 — Denied

Get: Denial reason, EOB/Denial Letter to MVA@enablecomp.com or fax 615-873-0959, appeal deadline and address.

  • Status: EOB Requested | Review: 15 Days

Scenario 5 — Not Received

Ask: Timely filing date? Billing address and fax? Request rebill.

"Called BCBS. Spoke with Mitchell. No bill received. Timely filing 180 days from discharge. Confirmed billing address and fax. Requesting resubmission."
  • Status: Request ReBill — Initial | Review: 1 Day

Quick Reference Table

Bill StatusMVA StatusCase Page StatusReview
ProcessingCOM Bill FiledCommercial Bill Filed30 Days
Pending PaymentCOM Bill FiledCommercial Bill Filed15 Days
PaidCOM PaidPayment Mailed15 Days
DeniedCOM Check Claim StatusEOB Requested15 Days
Not ReceivedCOM Check Claim StatusRequest ReBill — Initial1 Day

Third Party — Bill POR

Following up on third-party liability and attorney-represented claims

If the claimant is attorney-represented at any point, move claim to Validation Pending — Level 2.

Prerequisites

  • Patient must have a TPL claim and/or attorney representation
  • Claims must be notated and updated with all relevant info
  • Review for lien filing — confirm if lien was sent
  • Prior to follow-up, review prior notes and confirm lien documentation

Contact via Email

Send directly to claims adjuster. Subject: [ENCRYPT] Claim #: [number]

Claim #: {00000} | Date of Service: {DOS} | Balance: {amount} | Name: {patient} | Facility: {facility}

"I'm reaching out to obtain the status of the above claim. Our team has previously provided billing — could you confirm receipt? [If lien applicable:] A lien is on file — please advise if you need a copy. Please advise on most recent contact with claimant and if anything is needed from the hospital."
  • Status: Billed POR | Review: 3 Days

Contact via Phone

"Hello, this is [Name] from [Facility] to obtain bill status. This call is being recorded."

If Claim is Closed — Scenarios

Scenario 1 — Paid/Settled with Patient

Ask if medical funds set aside. If YES → inform adjuster where to remit. If NO → confirm BI settlement date. Add to Escalation Tracker. NRD: 30 Days.

Scenario 2 — Claim Denied

Get denial reason. Request EOB/denial letter to fax 615-873-0959 or MVA@enablecomp.com. Move to Return Review. NRD: 1 Day.

Scenario 3 — Patient Unresponsive/Lack of Interest

Send TPL Claim Letter to patient. Move to Return Review. NRD: 1 Day.

Scenario 4 — Settled with Attorney

Confirm settlement date and date of patient release. Add to Escalation Tracker. NRD: 30 Days.

If Claim is Open

Confirm receipt of bill and lien. If not received → get email/fax for resubmission. Then move to BSP and notate what needs to be sent. NRD: 60 Days.

After 2+ unsuccessful follow-ups, escalate to adjuster supervisor. If still unable — add to Escalation Tracker.

EOB Requested

Requesting Explanation of Benefits from payers

❗ If EOB is already on the Documents tab — do NOT call. Simply forward to EOB Review. NRD: 7 Days.

When to Request

Always attempt to obtain an EOB when working Bill Filed accounts, especially after a denial.

How to Request — Calling Payer

1Call payer's Provider Support Line.
2Request EOB (provide claim number / date of service).
3Confirm delivery method: mail, fax, or electronic.
"Called FP Progressive at 888-888-8888. Spoke with [Rep]. Claim denied for [reason]. Requested EOB via fax/email at MVA@enablecomp.com or 615-873-0959."
  • Status: EOB Requested | Review: 15 Days

Special Scenarios

SituationAction
Denial — Missing Medical RecordsMove to Request Rebill immediately. Add note. Do NOT wait for EOB.
Denial — Missing Info (already on file)Still request EOB for appeal. Keep status: EOB Requested.
EOB already sent by payerDocument date/destination. Move to EOB Review. NRD: Next Business Day.
Payer refuses (Third Party)Document refusal. Move to EOB Review. NRD: Next Business Day.
Portal access needed (no access)Document. Move to EOB Review. NRD: 1 Day.

Escalation

After 4 unsuccessful attempts → create GWF escalation. Document: all calls made, additional numbers tried, supervisor/adjuster escalation.

Follow-up Timeline

Follow up every 2 weeks if no response received.

Communication Standards

Professional communication requirements for all payer/patient contact

  • Answer calls promptly — no more than 3 rings when possible
  • Use a professional greeting and introduce yourself by name
  • Maintain an upbeat, professional tone at all times
  • Pause clearly when reading numerical values (billed charges, check amounts)
  • Use the full date format: Month Day, Year
  • Be cautious with abbreviations when communicating externally
  • Respect the communication preference of the recipient (phone or email)
If a patient is rude or unwilling to provide info, say: "We're just verifying your date of birth so we can confirm we're talking to the right person. If you don't feel comfortable, I completely understand — I'll send a letter to your address on file instead."

Patient Requests

  • If a patient requests a bill, advise them to contact the facility directly.
  • Always clarify you are NOT billing the patient directly — only submitting to their auto insurance.

Voicemail Guide

Scripts and NRD rules for voicemails

Updated NRD Rules

WorkflowVoicemail SuccessfulVoicemail Unsuccessful
Bill Filed / Commercial / Bill PORNRD: 2 DaysNRD: 1 Day
Patient PursuitNRD: 7 DaysNRD: 7 Days

Payer Voicemail Script

"Hello, this is [Your Name] calling from [Facility] to obtain the bill status for patient [Patient Name] with Policy Number [Subscriber ID] with a date of service of [##/##/##]. Billed Charge amount is [$00.00]. If you could please return my call at [Your Number] and reference Claim ID [##########]."

Voicemail Details to Include

  • Your Name & Facility Name
  • Patient Name & Subscriber ID
  • Date of Service & Billed Charge
  • Callback Number & Claim ID

Attorney Voicemail Script

"My name is [Name] calling from [Facility]. Our records indicate you may be representing [Patient Name] who was involved in an accident on [date]. Please confirm representation by sending a letter of representation and medical authorization to MVA@enablecomp.com or fax 615-486-6518."

Patient Voicemail Script

"Good Morning/Afternoon. My name is _____ calling from [Facility]. Please return my call at (615) 694-8131 ext 992566 and reference Claim ID _________. Thank you."

Compliance & Data Protection

HIPAA compliance and data protection requirements

  • Mark sensitive information for redaction when required
  • Follow HIPAA compliance when sharing patient data
  • Document all communications and account updates
  • Ensure billed charges match submitted claim data
All outbound/inbound email correspondence must be uploaded to Enforcer. All communication must remain professional.

Save to Related Feature

The "Save to Related" feature copies a note to all related claims not in closed/return status. If you only want to save to the displayed account, use the standard "Save" button.

FAQ / Additional Updates

Frequently asked questions and recent updates

FAQ

  • What is the Subscriber ID? — It is the claim number.
  • What fax template for TPL/BI fax submissions? — Use the liability claim packet template.
  • Policy number field required? — Not required. Only add if obtained from payer during validation.
  • Payer location not auto-selected? — Manually select the correct address from the dropdown. See cheat sheet.

Recent Updates

  • If RS needs to send or resubmit a bill to have an EOB → Move to Request Rebill — Initial. NRD: 7 Days.
  • If EOB is attached on Documents tab → Move to EOB Review. NRD: 10 Days.

Abbreviations

Quick reference for all MVA billing abbreviations

Abbr.Meaning
ICDInternational Classification of Disease
NPINational Provider Identifier
CPTCommon Procedural Terminology
HCPCSHealthcare Common Procedural Coding System
SMVASingle Motor Vehicle Accident
MMVAMultiple Motor Vehicle Accident
PMVAPedestrian Motor Vehicle Accident
PIPPersonal Injury Protection
UIMUnder Insured Motorist
UMUninsured Motorist
BIBodily Injury
COBCoordination of Benefits
TPLThird Party Liability
POSPlace of Service
DRGDiagnostic Related Codes
LORLetter of Representation
VRValidation Representative
GTMGlobal Team Member
MAMedical Authorization
BSPBill Submission Pending status
AOBAssignment of Benefits
CSClient System
ROIRelease of Information
HBHospital Bills
PBProfessional Bills
